title: Effect of slow bonds on current fluctuations in the symmetric simple exclusion process
authors: Soumyabrata Saha, Sandeep Jangid, Kapil Sharma, Tridib Sadhu
abstract: The symmetric simple exclusion process (SSEP) is a paradigmatic model of classical non-equilibrium dynamics. Exact results for large deviations of particle current in the SSEP have been obtained in various settings using integrability-based methods. In this Article, we discuss how these results are modified in the presence of localized slow bonds. We consider three conventional geometries: (a) a finite one-dimensional lattice weakly coupled to unequal reservoirs at its boundaries, (b) a semi-infinite one-dimensional lattice weakly coupled to a boundary reservoir, and (c) an infinite one-dimensional lattice with localized slow bonds near the origin. For each case, we present exact expressions for the large deviation function of current and validate them through rare-event simulations based on the cloning algorithm. In connection with our results, we present an elementary derivation of the exact large deviation function for the current in the semi-infinite SSEP, complementing recent results obtained through more elaborate techniques.
summary: This paper provides exact analytical solutions for how localized bottlenecks affect current fluctuations in the SSEP model. It establishes that while the system is robust to small defects, a critical scaling regime exists where the slow bonds fundamentally change the macroscopic transport statistics.
main_problem: Investigating how localized microscopic disorder, specifically 'slow bonds' with reduced hopping rates, modifies the large deviation statistics of particle current in the Symmetric Simple Exclusion Process (SSEP).
main_result: The authors derive exact expressions for the large deviation function of the current across three geometries (infinite, semi-infinite, and finite lattices) and identify a critical scaling regime where slow bonds act as a bottleneck.
method: The study uses an extension of Macroscopic Flutuation Theory (MFT) and fluctuating hydrodynamics combined with a new coarse-graining scheme, validated by rare-event simulations using the cloning algorithm.
model_or_system: The Symmetric Simple Exclusion Process (SSEP) on 1D lattices, where particles perform unbiased random walks with exclusion rules, featuring localized defect bonds with a reduced jump rate gamma.
key_observables: Large deviation function (LDF) and Scaled Cumulant Generating Function (SCGF) of the particle current (net particle flux).
important_parameters: Jump rate (gamma), number of slow bonds (l), system size (L), observation time (T), and the scaling parameter Gamma = gamma / sqrt(T) or gamma / L.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes that the large-scale properties can be captured by coarse-grained density fields and utilizes the additivity principle for decomposing the system.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a schematic of the SSEP lattice with defect bonds; Figures 2, 4, and 6 provide quantitative comparisons between theoretical SCGF results and numerical simulation data for different geometries.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of slow bonds, presents exact results for three different lattice geometries (infinite, semi-infinite, and finite), provides a new coarse-graining derivation, and validates the analytical findings with numerical cloning simulations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Infinite-line SSEP: The SSEP on an infinite integer lattice with ℓlocalized defect bonds (zigzag line) placed around the bond between sites 0 and 1. Symmetric jump rates across the bonds are indicated, which is γ for the defect bonds and 1 for the rest. The schematic shows an example with three defect bonds placed around the origin. The empty circles indicate unoccupied sites while the filled circles indicate sites occupied by a particle.

FIG. 2. Scgf for infinite SSEP: A quantitative comparison of the scgf (8) of the time integrated current QT across the origin for a half-filled (ρa(b) = 1/2) SSEP on an infinite one-dimensional lattice with ℓslow bonds with jump rates γ = Γ √

FIG. 3. Semi-infinite SSEP: SSEP on a positive integer lattice coupled to a reservoir of density ρa at the leftmost site through a bond whose jump rate is controlled by a parameter γ. In the bulk, jump rates are uniformly set to value one.

FIG. 4. Scgf for semi-infinite SSEP: (a) The scgf of current for the semi-infinite SSEP in the fast coupling regime (γ = 1) for densities (ρa, ρb) ≡(0.9, 0.1). The solid black line represents the theoretical result µsi(λ, ρa, ρb) = Rsi(ω(λ, ρa, ρb)) with (6), while the data points are obtained by a cloning algorithm with T = 500 and Nc = 50, 000. The dashed line represents the function 1
FIG. 5. Finite SSEP: SSEP on a finite lattice coupled to two boundary reservoirs of densities ρa and ρb with slow bonds.
